analysis.problem: Inadequate waste management and plastic pollution
analysis.root_cause: Lack of coordinated approaches and insufficient infrastructure for waste management and recycling
leverage_points: Corporate investments in sustainable practices and innovations

proposed_actions.action: Implement extended producer responsibility for plastics
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce plastic waste by 30% within the next 2 years

proposed_actions.action: Increase funding for waste management and recycling research
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Develop and deploy 2 new recycling technologies within the next 3 years

evidence: The Environmental Research & Education Foundation (EREF) raised over $4 million for waste and recycling research
Climate change is reshaping global plastic pollution, and a systems-level challenge requires coordinated approaches
analysis.generalization: Inadequate waste management and plastic pollution contribute to environmental degradation, human health risks, and economic losses, affecting the dignity of all individuals and the sustainability of the planet
analysis.summary: Inadequate waste management and plastic pollution are critical problems requiring immediate attention, and coordinated approaches from governments, corporations, and individuals are necessary to address these issues
